task_description: Plan Sprint 14 for the Nexus Analytics team. Sprint capacity is 34 story points. You have 13 items in the backlog — some already flagged P0/P1 by triage, others are P2 features and tech debt. Your job: select which items to commit to this sprint using 'schedule' actions (sprint=1), 'defer' anything you're pushing out, then 'submit'. Constraints: all P0 items MUST be included. You cannot exceed 34 points. Maximize the total value delivered.

context.quarter_goal: Reduce churn among enterprise customers by 15%
okrs: OKR2: Increase self-serve conversion rate by 10% (weight: 0.30)
OKR3: Reduce P1/P0 bug backlog to < 5 open items (weight: 0.20)
OKR4: Improve API developer experience NPS by 15pts (weight: 0.10)
hard_constraints: All P0-priority items must be scheduled
Items with dependencies must have their dependencies scheduled first or already done
soft_goals: Balance bug fixes with feature work
Address at least one tech debt item
Include at least one item per OKR if possible

items.title: SSO login fails for Okta integrations when MFA is enforced
items.description: Enterprise customers using Okta SSO with MFA enforcement are unable to log in.

items.title: Data pipeline silently drops rows with null timestamps
items.description: Enterprise compliance reports missing records weeks later due to silent row drops.

items.title: API rate limiter returns 429 for valid requests under documented limit
items.description: Sliding window bug causes false rate limit errors. Developer customers threatening churn.

items.title: Billing page 500 error when promo code field is blank
items.description: Checkout abandonment on blank promo code. Missing revenue from failed upgrades.

items.title: In-app alerts: threshold-based email/Slack notifications
items.description: Users want automatic alerts when a metric crosses a defined threshold. Top-requested feature in NPS surveys (mentioned by 34% of enterprise respondents). Requires notification service integration.

items.title: CSV import wizard with column mapping UI
items.description: Self-serve customers struggle to import custom CSVs. A guided wizard with drag-drop column mapping would reduce support tickets by an estimated 30% and improve activation.

items.title: Role-based access control (RBAC) — viewer/editor/admin
items.description: Enterprise deals are blocked by lack of RBAC. Top blocker in 6 active enterprise deals (combined TCV: $1.8M). Required for SOC2 compliance roadmap.

items.title: Migrate dashboard rendering from legacy D3 v4 to Recharts
items.description: D3 v4 is 7 years old and blocks us from adding modern chart types. Migration unlocks 3 future features and reduces bundle size 40%. No user-visible change on completion.

items.title: Add structured logging + distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry)
items.description: Current logs are unstructured strings. P0 incidents take 3x longer to diagnose. Adding OpenTelemetry enables faster MTTR and is a prerequisite for FEAT-104 (observability dashboard).

items.title: API usage observability dashboard for developer accounts
items.description: Developer customers want to see their API call volume, latency percentiles, and error rates in-app. Reduces support load and differentiates our API offering vs competitors.

items.title: Onboarding checklist shows 100% complete when 3 steps remain
items.description: New users think setup is done when it's not, reducing activation rate.

items.title: Scheduled reports not delivered to email + aliases
items.description: Valid RFC email aliases rejected by broken regex. Reports silently not sent.

items.title: Discovery: AI-powered anomaly detection — feasibility spike
items.description: Explore whether we can add ML-based anomaly detection to dashboards. 1-week spike to assess data requirements, model options, and infra cost. Not a deliverable, just research.

notes: Optimal: both P0s + high-value P1s that fit. FEAT-101 (13pts) is high value but leaves no room for P0s. FEAT-103 (21pts) alone exceeds half capacity and blocks everything. TECH-202 is P2 and has no direct user value this sprint.
